nuggets.headline: For "HUMBLE.", Mike WiLL Made-It's Hit Formula Was Filtered Through TDE's Sounwave
nuggets.text: The beat for "HUMBLE." represents a crucial fusion of two production worlds. Mike WiLL Made-It, fresh off producing for Beyoncé's "Lemonade," brought his influential, chart-dominant sound, while TDE's in-house staple Sounwave co-produced, ensuring the track fit within Kendrick's established sonic lineage. This collaboration allowed the song to function as a massive single while retaining the raw, in-house feel that has defined Kendrick's work since his earliest projects.

source.title: A Deep Dive Into How Kendrick Lamar Made 'DAMN.'
source.quoteSnippet: One of the more influential producers of his generation, Mike WiLL Made-It also has a Beyoncé Lemonade credit to his name... Sounwave, one of Top Dawg’s most prolific in-house producers and a member of the Digi+Phonics team, has worked with Kendrick since his earliest projects.

nuggets.headline: Listen For The Intentional Emptiness of the "Section.80" Sonic Philosophy
nuggets.text: The stark minimalism of "HUMBLE." is a direct, intentional pivot from the lush, live instrumentation of 2015's "To Pimp a Butterfly." Producer Terrace Martin confirmed that for "DAMN.", the creative team deliberately returned to the sonic ethos of Kendrick's 2011 album "Section.80." Listen for how the production is stripped to its core elements—a hard piano loop, aggressive drums, and little else—leaving maximum space for what Martin called "Kendrick rapping and writing."

source.title: A Deep Dive Into How Kendrick Lamar Made 'DAMN.'
source.quoteSnippet: Instead, they went back to Section.80 and started there. 'The essence, man. Samples, drums, just a little vibe, and just Kendrick rapping and writing,' Martin says.

nuggets.imageCaption: Kendrick Lamar performing live, channeling the raw energy present in "HUMBLE."

nuggets.headline: If the Confrontational Bars of "HUMBLE." Hit, Find Ab-Soul
nuggets.text: If you're drawn to Kendrick's aggressive, intricate lyricism on "HUMBLE.", you need to explore the work of his Black Hippy groupmate Ab-Soul. As another core member of the TDE camp, Ab-Soul shares Kendrick's penchant for dense wordplay and confrontational themes, but with a more esoteric and conspiratorial edge. His sharp, often abrasive delivery and complex rhyme structures deliver the same raw, intellectual energy that defines Kendrick's performance here.

source.title: Ab-Soul
source.quoteSnippet: Ab-Soul's music is known for its lyrical complexity and philosophical themes.

nuggets.imageCaption: TDE label mate and Black Hippy member Ab-Soul.

artistSummary: Kendrick Lamar is a Pulitzer Prize-winning artist celebrated for his dense, symbolic narratives. His work on albums like "DAMN." explores urgent themes of religion, anxiety, and politics with unparalleled lyrical depth.
